title: Shorten the ACL-probe local username so it fits Windows' 20-char limit
action: In Assert-SecondUserDenied (scripts/windows-runtime-smoke.ps1, currently line 175), change the local-user name prefix from "herdr_go_acl_" (13 chars) to "herdr_acl_" (10 chars) so the full generated name (prefix + 8-char GUID suffix) is 18 characters, safely under Windows' 20-character New-LocalUser name limit. This is a live regression: commit 57c72174 (binary-rename-herdr-go-1) lengthened the prefix from the previously-fitting "herdctl_acl_" (12 chars) without checking the 20-char budget, so New-LocalUser now always throws "Cannot validate argument on parameter 'Name'" and the second-user token-isolation smoke can never pass - confirmed live on a real windows-2022 GitHub Actions run just now. Do not touch anything else in the file - this is a one-line string literal change.

truths: The generated local username (prefix + 8-char GUID suffix) is at most 20 characters
No other line in scripts/windows-runtime-smoke.ps1 changes

artifacts.substantive: Assert-SecondUserDenied's $user prefix is 10 characters or fewer, still recognizably product-named

prohibitions: Do not change the GUID suffix length, the password generation, the credential construction, or the read/denial probe logic
Do not touch any other function in this script

verify:
python3 -c '
text = open("scripts/windows-runtime-smoke.ps1").read()
assert "herdr_go_acl_" not in text, "old over-length prefix still present"
import re
m = re.search(r"user = \"(herdr[a-zA-Z_]*_)\$\(\[Guid\]", text)
assert m, "user prefix pattern not found"
prefix = m.group(1)
assert len(prefix) + 8 <= 20, f"prefix {prefix!r} ({len(prefix)} chars) + 8 exceeds 20"
print("OK", prefix, len(prefix) + 8)
'

trace.outcome: Shortened ACL-probe username prefix from herdr_go_acl_ (13 chars) to herdr_acl_ (10 chars) so the generated name fits Windows' 20-char New-LocalUser limit
trace.verify_command: python3 -c "text = open(\"scripts/windows-runtime-smoke.ps1\").read(); assert \"herdr_go_acl_\" not in text; import re; m = re.search(r\"user = .(herdr[a-zA-Z_]*_)\\\$\\(\\[Guid\\]\", text); prefix = m.group(1); assert len(prefix) + 8 <= 20; print(\"OK\", prefix, len(prefix) + 8)"
trace.reopened_reason: lane created, cell content already correct, reopening rather than recreating
